subject: Anthropology
title: Oldowan Tools
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Oldowan Tools.

hook.text: Strike one rock against another at the right angle. A sharp flake chips off. Technology is born.

buildup.text: Oldowan tools are the oldest known stone tools, dating to about 2.6 million years ago in East Africa.

discovery.text: Simple choppers and flakes were used to cut meat, scrape hides, and crack bones for energy-rich marrow.

twist.text: Making them required planning and skill. The toolmaker had to choose the right rock and strike precisely.

climax.text: Oldowan tools mark the moment hominins began shaping the world instead of just living in it.

punchline.text: One sharp edge started two million years of innovation.

quiz.question: What were Oldowan tools primarily used for?
